metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the `docs/architecture.md` remedy row for `inbound_differ.py` (currently pointing to `inbound_status.py` / `_jira_status_to_local`) is superseded by this plan's `inbound_fields.py` seam, and update the plan to either acknowledge the divergence or explain why `inbound_fields.py` is the correct seam vs. the originally-documented `inbound_status.py` split.
evidence: Plan: 'Graduate it off the allowlist by splitting on the call-graph seam docs/architecture.md already records: the hand-maintained Jira->local field/status translation layer.'
docs/architecture.md line 265: remedy row says split into `inbound_status.py` with `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS` + `_jira_status_to_local and friends`
ABSENCE: `_jira_status_to_local` does not appear anywhere in `inbound_differ.py` (805 lines read in full)
Plan proposes `inbound_fields.py` with a materially broader set of symbols than the architecture.md remedy row describes
findings.finding: The plan's proposed split seam (`inbound_fields.py`) diverges from the remedy row recorded in `docs/architecture.md`, which describes a different seam: extracting into `inbound_status.py` with `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS` + `_jira_status_to_local and friends`. The function `_jira_status_to_local` does not exist in `inbound_differ.py` at all, confirming the architecture.md row is describing a different (apparently superseded or aspirational) split. The plan says to 'delete its remedy row' — which is correct — but this discrepancy means the plan should acknowledge the architectural record disagreement rather than silently deleting a row that points to a different target.
findings.location: ## What / plan claim 'docs/architecture.md already records' the seam; docs/architecture.md line 265
scenarios: A reviewer checking the architecture.md remedy row against the plan's deliverable will find the two diverge — the named split target file (`inbound_status.py`) and the named function (`_jira_status_to_local`) both differ from the plan's proposal, raising questions about whether the plan is implementing the right seam or the architecture.md was already stale before this ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence in the ## Why or ## Notes section: 'The architecture.md remedy row described an earlier seam (`inbound_status.py` / `_jira_status_to_local`); that seam was superseded — `_jira_status_to_local` was never extracted and the broader `inbound_fields.py` cluster is the correct seam. The remedy row is deleted (not updated) as part of graduation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale explaining why `inbound_fields.py` (broader scope) is preferred over the `inbound_status.py` split prescribed in `docs/architecture.md` line 265, or align the plan with the architecture.md prescription.
evidence: Plan: 'split on the call-graph seam docs/architecture.md already records: the hand-maintained Jira->local field/status translation layer'

docs/architecture.md line 265: 'Split along the existing seam: the hand-maintained `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS` map + status-translation helpers (`_jira_status_to_local` and friends) into a sibling `inbound_status.py`'

Plan moves: _load_adf, _ADF_KEY_INBOUND, _AdfModule_Inbound, _extract_jira_field_value, _assignee_matches, _normalize_jira_body, _map_jira_to_local_fields — none of these are named in the architecture.md seam description
ABSENCE: no stated rationale for choosing 'inbound_fields.py' over 'inbound_status.py', or for the wider extraction scope
findings.finding: The plan's positive rationale claims it splits 'on the call-graph seam docs/architecture.md already records,' but the architecture.md remedy row (line 265) prescribes a different module name ('inbound_status.py') and a narrower scope (status map + status-translation helpers only). The plan extracts a broader cluster (also _load_adf, _extract_jira_field_value, _assignee_matches, _normalize_jira_body, _map_jira_to_local_fields) into 'inbound_fields.py' without explaining why this wider cut and different name are preferred over the recorded seam.
findings.location: ## Why / ## What — rationale + symbol list; docs/architecture.md line 265
scenarios: Reviewer or future developer reads architecture.md expecting the seam to follow its prescription; instead finds a different module name and scope, making the docs unreliable.
A follow-on splitter of the remaining helpers expects 'inbound_status.py' to already exist and is confused when it doesn't.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AC3 verify command to also confirm docs/architecture.md no longer references the remedy row for inbound_differ (e.g. append `docs/architecture.md` to the grep target list).
evidence: AC3 text: 'its docs/architecture.md remedy row is deleted'
AC3 verify: '! grep inbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt' — docs/architecture.md is absent from the grep invocation
findings.finding: AC3's verify command (`! grep inbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt`) does not check docs/architecture.md, even though the AC text explicitly requires the remedy row in that file to be deleted. The docs/architecture.md deletion is claimed but has no proving command.
scenarios: An executor deletes the allowlist rows but forgets to remove the docs/architecture.md remedy row; AC3's verify command passes falsely, leaving stale documentation.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the verify command to: `! grep inbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt docs/architecture.md`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC3's verify step to also confirm the `docs/architecture.md` remedy row is gone (e.g. add `&& ! grep -A1 'inbound_differ' docs/architecture.md | grep -i remedy` or a prose 'manual verify' note).
evidence: AC3 text: 'inbound_differ.py is absent from BOTH .github/module-size-allowlist.txt and .github/module-size-ceilings.txt, and its docs/architecture.md remedy row is deleted'
AC3 verify: '! grep inbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt' — docs/architecture.md is not checked
findings.finding: AC3's verify command (`! grep inbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt`) does not verify the third action the criterion's own text describes: deletion of the remedy row in `docs/architecture.md`. The criterion's text names three artifacts to clean up, but its verify step only checks two.
scenarios: Implementer satisfies the verify command but forgets to remove the docs/architecture.md remedy row, leaving stale documentation that contradicts the graduated state.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3 verify: `&& ! grep 'inbound_differ' docs/architecture.md`

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).

verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_fields.py`)` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_fields.py` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_differ.py`)` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_differ.py` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `! grep inbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt` greps the unanchored word 'inbound_differ' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_inbound_differ'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"inbound_differ":')
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update any prose in `docs/architecture.md` that attributes the Jira->local field/status translation layer to `inbound_differ.py` to reference `inbound_fields.py` as the canonical owner of that layer (not just delete the remedy row).
evidence: Plan says: 'delete its remedy row in docs/architecture.md'
Plan also says: 'the hand-maintained Jira->local field/status translation layer' is a seam 'docs/architecture.md already records' — implying prose in that doc references this layer
ABSENCE: no step to update that prose to name inbound_fields.py as the new module implementing the seam
findings.finding: The plan removes only the 'remedy row' from docs/architecture.md but does not explicitly update any prose in that file that currently attributes the Jira->local translation layer to inbound_differ.py — after the split, inbound_fields.py is the canonical home of that layer, so any description pointing to inbound_differ.py for field/status translation becomes a stale reference.
findings.location: ## What — 'delete its remedy row in docs/architecture.md' / docs/architecture.md
scenarios: A developer reads docs/architecture.md to find where field translation lives and is directed to inbound_differ.py, which no longer contains that logic — they search there and must trace imports back to inbound_fields.py
findings.suggested_fix: Add a step: 'In docs/architecture.md, update the description of the Jira->local translation seam to name inbound_fields.py (not inbound_differ.py) as the implementing module.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC6 that imports each re-exported symbol and asserts it is non-None, e.g. `python -c "from rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler import inbound_differ; assert all(hasattr(inbound_differ, s) for s in ['_map_jira_to_local_fields','_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS','_assignee_matches','_normalize_jira_body'])"` (or a dedicated test).
evidence: AC6 text: 'inbound_differ._map_jira_to_local_fields, inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS, inbound_differ._assignee_matches, inbound_differ._normalize_jira_body still resolve as module attributes (re-exported)'
ABSENCE: no `python -c`, `pytest`, or other proving command is listed for this criterion
findings.finding: AC6 (backward compatibility: re-exported symbols still resolve as module attributes) states a specific, verifiable outcome but provides no proving command. Without a command, an executor cannot confirm re-exports work at import time.
scenarios: An executor adds re-export lines but makes a typo in one name; no command in the plan would catch it before the change is merged.
findings.suggested_fix: python -c "from rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler import inbound_differ; syms = ['_map_jira_to_local_fields','_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS','_assignee_matches','_normalize_jira_body']; missing = [s for s in syms if not hasattr(inbound_differ, s)]; assert not missing, f'Missing re-exports: {missing}'"

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand AC6 to enumerate ALL moved symbols that must remain resolvable as `inbound_differ.<symbol>` attributes (or confirm which subset is intentionally excluded from compat guarantees and document why).
evidence: What section lists 8 symbols to move: '_load_adf', '_ADF_KEY_INBOUND', '_AdfModule_Inbound', '_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_TYPE', '_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_PRIORITY', '_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS', '_REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL', '_extract_jira_field_value', '_assignee_matches', '_normalize_jira_body', '_map_jira_to_local_fields'
AC6 only names 4: '_map_jira_to_local_fields', '_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS', '_assignee_matches', '_normalize_jira_body'
findings.finding: AC6 verifies backward compatibility for only 4 of the 8+ symbols the What section says will be moved and re-exported (`_load_adf`, `_ADF_KEY_INBOUND`, `_AdfModule_Inbound`, `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_TYPE`, `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_PRIORITY`, `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_PRIORITY`, `_extract_jira_field_value` are not listed). Described work — re-exporting the full translation cluster — has only partial criterion coverage.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6; What section (moved symbols list)
scenarios: An implementer accidentally omits a re-export for `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_TYPE` or `_extract_jira_field_value`; the verify step in AC6 passes anyway because it only checks the four listed symbols, leaving a latent AttributeError for any caller that imports those names from inbound_differ.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the symbols proposed for `inbound_fields.py` should instead be extracted into/consolidated with the existing `inbound_translate.py` (which already owns parallel `_JIRA_TYPE_MAP`, `_REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL`, `_JIRA_PRIORITY_MAP`, `_normalize_adf_body`) rather than creating a third Jira→local translation module; document the decision in the plan.
evidence: inbound_translate.py:53-88 defines `_JIRA_TYPE_MAP`, `_REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL`, `_JIRA_PRIORITY_MAP` — parallel to inbound_differ.py:136-169
inbound_translate.py:350-360 defines `_normalize_adf_body` — functionally identical to inbound_differ.py:390-397 `_normalize_jira_body`
inbound_translate.py:107-116 defines `_resolve_priority` using `_JIRA_PRIORITY_MAP` — parallel to inbound_differ.py `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_PRIORITY` usage
inbound_translate.py:284-293 defines `_extract_name` — structurally parallel to `_extract_jira_field_value`
The plan makes no mention of `inbound_translate.py` and does not address the existing duplication
docs/architecture.md:265 describes the planned remedy seam as splitting into `inbound_status.py`, not `inbound_fields.py`, suggesting the scope has drifted from the recorded remedy
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `inbound_fields.py` as the home for Jira→local translation logic, but `inbound_translate.py` already exists in the same package and owns a nearly-parallel set of Jira→local translation symbols: `_JIRA_TYPE_MAP` (≡ `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_TYPE`), `_REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL` (byte-identical copy), `_JIRA_PRIORITY_MAP` (≡ `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_PRIORITY`), `_normalize_adf_body` (≡ `_normalize_jira_body`), `_resolve_priority`, `_extract_name` (≡ `_extract_jira_field_value`). Creating a third Jira→local translation locus without consolidating or absorbing the existing duplication between `inbound_differ.py` and `inbound_translate.py` rebuilds functionality already present in the codebase.
scenarios: A developer maintaining `_REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL` must now update it in three places: `inbound_differ.py` → `inbound_fields.py` (new), `inbound_translate.py`, and `config.py` — worsening the existing duplication instead of resolving it.
A future reviewer sees three near-identical Jira→local maps and cannot determine which is authoritative.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the plan that `inbound_differ.py` must `from inbound_fields import _normalize_jira_body` (not just re-export it) so that `_diff_comments_inbound`'s direct call to `_normalize_jira_body` at line 456 continues to resolve — or reconsider whether `_normalize_jira_body` belongs in the moved cluster vs. staying with its second caller.
evidence: inbound_differ.py line 390: `def _normalize_jira_body(body: Any) -> str:` — listed as moved
inbound_differ.py line 456: `body_text = _normalize_jira_body(jc.get("body"))` — called inside `_diff_comments_inbound`
inbound_differ.py line 400: `def _diff_comments_inbound(...)` — listed as STAYING
Plan: 'a cohesive, one-way-dependency cluster — the new module references nothing that stays behind'
findings.finding: `_normalize_jira_body` is listed in the plan as part of the moved 'translation cluster', but it is also called by `_diff_comments_inbound` (line 456 of `inbound_differ.py`), which the plan explicitly says STAYS. The plan claims the moved cluster is 'the new module references nothing that stays behind' — which is true of inbound_fields.py's imports — but does not address the reverse dependency: the staying `_diff_comments_inbound` calls `_normalize_jira_body`. After the split, `inbound_differ.py` must import `_normalize_jira_body` back from `inbound_fields.py` to satisfy `_diff_comments_inbound`. The plan's re-export mechanism covers the attribute-access compatibility requirement, but the plan does not explicitly state that `_normalize_jira_body` will be imported back into `inbound_differ.py`'s namespace for use by `_diff_comments_inbound`.
findings.location: ## What — 'Moved symbols' list / 'the new module references nothing that stays behind'
scenarios: The implementer moves `_normalize_jira_body` to `inbound_fields.py` but does not add a local import in `inbound_differ.py` for its use in `_diff_comments_inbound` — causing a `NameError` at runtime when `_diff_comments_inbound` is called.

The re-export via `from inbound_fields import _normalize_jira_body` satisfies the AC#6 attribute-access test but a bare function call inside `_diff_comments_inbound` still needs it in the module namespace.
findings.suggested_fix: In ## What, append: 'Note: `_normalize_jira_body` is also called by `_diff_comments_inbound` (stays). The re-export `from inbound_fields import _normalize_jira_body` in `inbound_differ.py` satisfies both the backward-compat AC and the internal call.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Address the relationship between the new `inbound_fields.py` and the existing `inbound_translate.py` (which already contains overlapping Jira→local translation maps and helpers): either consolidate into `inbound_translate.py` instead of creating a new file, or document why a separate leaf module is preferable and add a parity assertion between the two.
evidence: src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py lines 53-88: _JIRA_TYPE_MAP, _REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL, _JIRA_PRIORITY_MAP — duplicate of _JIRA_TO_LOCAL_TYPE, _REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL, _JIRA_TO_LOCAL_PRIORITY in inbound_differ.py
inbound_translate.py line 67: '_REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL mirrors inbound_differ._REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL'
ABSENCE: plan has no mention of inbound_translate.py or how the new inbound_fields.py relates to it
inbound_translate.py line 350-360: _normalize_adf_body mirrors _normalize_jira_body being moved
findings.finding: An existing sibling module `inbound_translate.py` already implements Jira→local field translation (type map `_JIRA_TYPE_MAP`, priority map `_JIRA_PRIORITY_MAP`, status map `_REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL`, `_normalize_adf_body`, `_extract_name`, `_jira_status_to_local`). The plan creates a new `inbound_fields.py` with an overlapping translation cluster without addressing this existing file, risking two diverging sources of truth for Jira→local translation constants.
scenarios: A future maintainer adds a new Jira status mapping and updates inbound_fields.py, missing the duplicate in inbound_translate.py (or vice versa), re-introducing the status drift the parity test was built to catch.
Code review of the new inbound_fields.py questions the duplication with inbound_translate.py; implementer has no plan guidance on how to respond.
